goal: Refine the isolated comparison PR into a direct consumer card experience with program-level plans, benefits, and tier-aware comparisons.
completed: Removed the Confirm with issuer, reported/observed, regional-normalization, publication-boundary, and eligibility/economics language from normal public browsing and comparison surfaces.
Removed the regional scope callout and comparison methodology strip, plus the profile publication-boundary strip.
Modeled one catalog entry per card program with optional plans and plan-scoped facts and benefits; program slugs remain stable.

Ready now appears once as Ready Card with URL-addressable Lite and Metal choices that update cost, FX, rewards, ATM terms, card format, benefits, profile Compare state, and comparison values together.
Added source-backed Ready travel, rewards, partner, subscription, and wallet benefits and Wirex cashback, subscription-refund, wallet, and five-tier summary details.
Added Benefits & perks to profiles and benefits rows to the semantic comparison table.
Kept the mobile comparison as a localized horizontal table with a sticky factor column and compact plan controls inside program columns.
Updated the CardStats UI blueprint and persisted design system with the program-plan-benefit interaction contract.
nextActions: Refresh and smoke-test the protected Vercel preview, then review pull request 6; merge it if accepted or close it without changing main if rejected.
Source exact tier economics before enabling selectors for Wirex, Crypto.com, COCA, Cypher, Deblock, KAST, and other tiered programs.
decisions: The catalog unit is a card program, not a card-plan combination; Ready Lite and Ready Metal are not separate list entries.
Plan selection lives directly below the profile header and inside the relevant comparison column, and it travels in shareable URL state.
Benefits & perks is the durable consumer label. Promotions are time-bound benefits and must carry dates and conditions when added.
A tier selector is enabled only when plan-specific comparable values exist; showing tier names without their economics is not useful.
Public product copy uses direct labels such as Rewards, Regions, Visit website, View current terms, and Card details while keeping source links available.
Ready and Wirex additions use current official product/help pages checked on 18 July 2026; no unsupported Solflare discount claim was added.

passed: npm run check passes ESLint, strict TypeScript, 9 Vitest files with 24 tests including database publication integration, Prisma generation, and the Next.js production build.

Full Playwright suite passes 12 desktop/mobile tests covering Ready Lite/Metal switching, benefits, plan-aware comparison URLs, picker behavior, axe, reduced motion, localized scrolling, 375px, landscape, and 200% text.
Desktop Ready Metal profile and 375px Ready/Wirex/MetaMask tier comparison were visually inspected against the persisted design system.
The required ui-ux-pro-max validation search and accessibility, touch, responsive, interaction, and Next.js guidance were applied.
The frontend taste redesign audit informed direct copy, program-first hierarchy, sparse benefit layout, and the no-duplicate-card decision.
An initial concurrent final run hit a Playwright/ESLint test-results directory race; the sequential npm run check rerun and four focused desktop/mobile browser regressions passed.
notRun: Manual VoiceOver and NVDA testing is unavailable in this environment; native semantics, keyboard behavior, focus management, and axe passed.
The existing protected preview has not yet been refreshed with this worktree revision; production remains untouched.

risks: Exact tier thresholds and plan economics still need official-source work for most multi-tier programs; the UI intentionally does not invent selectors for them.
Ready terms and benefits can change; profile and comparison link directly to current Ready terms, but automated revalidation is not yet configured for this module.
Profile routes are server-rendered on demand after adding shareable plan query state; they remain indexable but are no longer emitted as individual static routes in the build summary.
Native dialog behavior passed Chromium automation but still needs manual VoiceOver and NVDA confirmation before production release.
doNotRepeat: Do not split one card program into duplicate catalog entries merely because it has Lite, Metal, stake, or membership plans.
Do not expose a tier selector until plan-specific fees, rewards, requirements, and benefits are sourced.
Do not use reported, observed, Confirm with issuer, or internal publication language in normal consumer UI.
Do not replace the mobile comparison table with card grids or allow it to create horizontal page overflow.
Do not deploy to production or merge this branch unless the user approves the pull-request result.
